title: Elatostema corniculatum
intro: Elatostema corniculatum, commonly known as the "Asian polypody" or "crawling shield fern," is a type of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema corniculatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Elatostema corniculatum is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Elatostema corniculatum is classified under the plant family Urticaceae. This family is commonly known as the nettle family. Elatostema corniculatum is a type of flowering plant within the genus Elatostema. The genus Elatostema is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Elatostema corniculatum is a species within this genus, characterized by its unique leaf and flower morphology.
